page: Amazon Web Services c6in.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with dedicated CPU allocation and hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU capabilities. The instance achieves top 25% performance in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU extended instructions. In software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing, the instance also registers in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, falls within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks remain in the average tier, reflecting the DDR4 memory generation.

The c6in.16xlarge is configured for workloads requiring high network throughput and parallel compute capacity, such as static web serving and database operations, where it demonstrates top 25% benchmark performance. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff, offering lower memory density per core compared to general-purpose or memory-optimized instances. This makes it less suitable for memory-bound applications, though it maintains average performance in LLM inference tasks and Redis SET operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and software compilation tests, while single-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is configured for network-heavy, compute-intensive workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations, presenting a tradeoff of lower memory density per core.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core CPU performance for network-intensive workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores, high-speed network bandwidth, and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for parallel workloads.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
